verses.text: He gives the twelve power over devils and to heal, and sends them to preach the kingdom. Sent forth. He shares His work and power with His own.
He gives the twelve power over devils and to heal, and sends them to preach the kingdom. Sent forth. He shares His work and power with His own.
Take nothing for the journey; shake off the dust where refused; they preach and heal. Travel light. They go leaning on God's provision.
Take nothing for the journey; shake off the dust where refused; they preach and heal. Travel light. They go leaning on God's provision.
Take nothing for the journey; shake off the dust where refused; they preach and heal. Travel light. They go leaning on God's provision.
Take nothing for the journey; shake off the dust where refused; they preach and heal. Travel light. They go leaning on God's provision.
Herod, perplexed, wonders if John be risen, and desires to see Jesus. Troubled conscience. The murdered prophet haunts the guilty king.
Herod, perplexed, wonders if John be risen, and desires to see Jesus. Troubled conscience. The murdered prophet haunts the guilty king.
Herod, perplexed, wonders if John be risen, and desires to see Jesus. Troubled conscience. The murdered prophet haunts the guilty king.
The apostles return; He takes them apart, but the crowds follow, and He heals them. No rest from mercy. He receives the people even when seeking quiet.
The apostles return; He takes them apart, but the crowds follow, and He heals them. No rest from mercy. He receives the people even when seeking quiet.
The disciples would send the crowd away; 'Give ye them to eat'; they have but five loaves and two fishes. Small store. He bids them serve with the little they have.
The disciples would send the crowd away; 'Give ye them to eat'; they have but five loaves and two fishes. Small store. He bids them serve with the little they have.
The disciples would send the crowd away; 'Give ye them to eat'; they have but five loaves and two fishes. Small store. He bids them serve with the little they have.
He blesses and breaks; all five thousand are filled, twelve baskets over. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little is more than enough.
He blesses and breaks; all five thousand are filled, twelve baskets over. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little is more than enough.
He blesses and breaks; all five thousand are filled, twelve baskets over. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little is more than enough.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'The Christ of God.' The confession. Here is the truth on which faith rests.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'The Christ of God.' The confession. Here is the truth on which faith rests.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'The Christ of God.' The confession. Here is the truth on which faith rests.
He foretells the Son of man must suffer, be slain, and rise the third day. The cross ahead. The Christ is also the suffering Saviour.
He foretells the Son of man must suffer, be slain, and rise the third day. The cross ahead. The Christ is also the suffering Saviour.
Let him deny himself and 'take up his cross daily'; what is a man profited if he lose himself? Daily dying. To follow Christ is a daily surrender of self.
Let him deny himself and 'take up his cross daily'; what is a man profited if he lose himself? Daily dying. To follow Christ is a daily surrender of self.
Let him deny himself and 'take up his cross daily'; what is a man profited if he lose himself? Daily dying. To follow Christ is a daily surrender of self.
Whoso is ashamed of Me, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed. Open allegiance. To own Christ now is to be owned by Him then.
Whoso is ashamed of Me, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed. Open allegiance. To own Christ now is to be owned by Him then.
On the mount He is transfigured, and Moses and Elias speak of His 'decease' at Jerusalem. Glory and the cross. Even in glory, His death is the theme.
On the mount He is transfigured, and Moses and Elias speak of His 'decease' at Jerusalem. Glory and the cross. Even in glory, His death is the theme.
On the mount He is transfigured, and Moses and Elias speak of His 'decease' at Jerusalem. Glory and the cross. Even in glory, His death is the theme.
On the mount He is transfigured, and Moses and Elias speak of His 'decease' at Jerusalem. Glory and the cross. Even in glory, His death is the theme.
Peter, half awake, would build three tents, 'not knowing what he said.' Confused zeal. He would hold the glory and miss the purpose.
Peter, half awake, would build three tents, 'not knowing what he said.' Confused zeal. He would hold the glory and miss the purpose.
A cloud overshadows; 'This is my beloved Son: hear him'; they see Jesus alone. Hear the Son. Above all others, Christ is to be heard.
A cloud overshadows; 'This is my beloved Son: hear him'; they see Jesus alone. Hear the Son. Above all others, Christ is to be heard.
A cloud overshadows; 'This is my beloved Son: hear him'; they see Jesus alone. Hear the Son. Above all others, Christ is to be heard.
A man begs for his only son, whom the disciples could not heal. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.
A man begs for his only son, whom the disciples could not heal. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.
A man begs for his only son, whom the disciples could not heal. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.
A man begs for his only son, whom the disciples could not heal. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.
'O faithless... generation'; He rebukes the devil and heals the child; all are amazed at God's power. Mighty mercy. He does at once what unbelief could not.
'O faithless... generation'; He rebukes the devil and heals the child; all are amazed at God's power. Mighty mercy. He does at once what unbelief could not.
'O faithless... generation'; He rebukes the devil and heals the child; all are amazed at God's power. Mighty mercy. He does at once what unbelief could not.
The Son of man shall be delivered to men; they understand not, and fear to ask. Slow to learn. They cannot grasp a suffering Messiah.
The Son of man shall be delivered to men; they understand not, and fear to ask. Slow to learn. They cannot grasp a suffering Messiah.
Disputing who is greatest, He sets a child by Him; 'he that is least... shall be great.' True greatness. Humility, not rank, is honoured.
Disputing who is greatest, He sets a child by Him; 'he that is least... shall be great.' True greatness. Humility, not rank, is honoured.
Disputing who is greatest, He sets a child by Him; 'he that is least... shall be great.' True greatness. Humility, not rank, is honoured.
John forbade one casting out devils; 'forbid him not: for he that is not against us is for us.' No narrowness. Christ owns service done in His name beyond our circle.
John forbade one casting out devils; 'forbid him not: for he that is not against us is for us.' No narrowness. Christ owns service done in His name beyond our circle.
He sets His face to Jerusalem; a Samaritan village will not receive Him. Resolute. He goes steadfastly toward the cross.
He sets His face to Jerusalem; a Samaritan village will not receive Him. Resolute. He goes steadfastly toward the cross.
He sets His face to Jerusalem; a Samaritan village will not receive Him. Resolute. He goes steadfastly toward the cross.
James and John would call down fire; 'ye know not what... spirit ye are of'; He came to save. Wrong zeal. The Saviour's heart is mercy, not vengeance.
James and John would call down fire; 'ye know not what... spirit ye are of'; He came to save. Wrong zeal. The Saviour's heart is mercy, not vengeance.
James and John would call down fire; 'ye know not what... spirit ye are of'; He came to save. Wrong zeal. The Saviour's heart is mercy, not vengeance.
One offers to follow; 'the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.' Count the cost. To follow Christ is to share His homelessness.
One offers to follow; 'the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.' Count the cost. To follow Christ is to share His homelessness.
Let me first bury my father; 'let the dead bury their dead'; no man looking back 'is fit for the kingdom.' Wholehearted. The plough of discipleship allows no backward glance.
Let me first bury my father; 'let the dead bury their dead'; no man looking back 'is fit for the kingdom.' Wholehearted. The plough of discipleship allows no backward glance.
Let me first bury my father; 'let the dead bury their dead'; no man looking back 'is fit for the kingdom.' Wholehearted. The plough of discipleship allows no backward glance.
